title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
slides: Google just sued a Chinese cybercrime ring. Why now?
The group used AI to scam hundreds of thousands globally.
Their deepfake voices and chatbots fooled even cautious users.
You now face AI-driven fraud that mimics real contacts.
Verify unexpected requests via a second channel before acting.
thread_text:
Google just sued a cybercrime ring. Why now?

The group used AI to create deepfake voices and chatbots that fooled hundreds of thousands globally.

Their tech mimicked real contacts closely.

Even cautious users couldn't tell the difference between a fake call and a real one from a friend.

The scale is staggering.

Hundreds of thousands were scammed across multiple countries, not just a few isolated incidents.

Here's the twist: the criminals were Chinese.

The ring operated from China, making cross-border prosecution complex and drawn-out.

Verify unexpected requests via a second channel before acting.

Call the person back on their known number. Don't trust a voice or text alone.
video_script: AI deepfakes fooled hundreds of thousands.
Verify via second channel before acting.

script: AI deepfakes fooled hundreds of thousands.
Verify via second channel before acting.

instagram.caption:
A single cybercrime ring used AI to clone voices and create chatbots, scamming hundreds of thousands of people before Google filed a lawsuit this week.  

The group mimicked real contacts so convincingly that even cautious users were tricked. The scale is global, not just isolated incidents.  

If you create or share content, a message or call that looks like you could be weaponized against your audience or followers.  

When was the last time you verified an urgent request from a friend by calling them back on a number you already saved? 📞🤖🛡️

carousel_slides.hook: AI cloned their voices. Now what?
carousel_slides.description: A Chinese cybercrime ring used AI deepfakes to scam hundreds of thousands globally, fooling even cautious users.
The ring's AI-generated voices and chatbots mimicked real contacts so well victims couldn't tell fake from real.
The massive scale involved victims across multiple countries, not just isolated incidents.
The ring operated from China, making legal action complex and slow for global authorities.

carousel_slides.hook: Save this to protect your money.
carousel_slides.description: Always verify unexpected requests by calling back on a known number, not trusting voice or text alone.
